name: Arni Fricasse (Lamb Fricasse)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy-based saucepan or Dutch oven, gently fry onion in butter until transparent.
2. Increase heat and add cubed lamb. Cook, stirring constantly, until meat juices evaporate. The meat should not brown.
3. Reduce heat and add hot water, herbs, salt, and pepper to taste. Cover and simmer gently for 1 to 1.5 hours.
4. Add prepared vegetable and continue cooking until lamb and vegetables are tender.
5. Carefully drain liquid from pan into a measuring jug and make up to 1.5 cups with hot water or stock. Keep contents hot.
6. Make Egg and Lemon Sauce:
- Bring stock to the boil.
- Mix cornflour with a little cold water to form a paste, then add to stock, stirring until thickened and bubbling. Let it boil for 1 minute.
- In a bowl, beat egg whites until stiff, add egg yolks, and continue beating until light and fluffy. Gradually pour in lemon juice while beating constantly.
- Gradually pour in boiling, thickened stock, beating constantly. Return sauce to the pan and cook over low heat for 1 to 2 minutes to cook the eggs. Do not allow sauce to boil. Remove from heat and continue stirring for 1 minute. Season to taste.
7. Pour Egg and Lemon Sauce over lamb and vegetables, cover, and leave at the side of the stove for 5 minutes.
8. Arrange lamb and vegetables on a serving dish and sprinkle with chopped herb. Serve immediately with crusty bread and a chilled white wine.
Note: Use any one of the following for the vegetable:
- 8-12 small globe artichoke hearts. Add to meat about 1 hour and cook for an additional 30-45 minutes.
- 500 g (1 lb) celery stalks, cut into 8 cm (3 inch) lengths, blanched in boiling salted water for 5 minutes. Drain, add to meat after 1 hour and cook for another 45 minutes.
- 4 heads of endive, washed well, trimmed of coarse leaves, slit in half lengthways, blanched in boiling salted water for 2 minutes, drained, and added to meat after 1.5 hours and cooked for an additional 15 minutes.
- 4 small firm heads of lettuce, washed well, quartered, scalded with boiling water, placed in a colander, and then added to meat after 1.5 hours and cooked for another 15 minutes.
From: "The Complete Middle East Cookbook" by Tess Mallos. ISBN: 1-86302-069
